models.name: MiniMax: MiniMax M2.7
models.comparative_rationale: Strong multi-turn tool use lineage with MiniMax M1 posting 0.620 airline tau-bench and competitive retail scores, supporting high agentic placement [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/function-calling-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: Xiaomi: MiMo-V2-Pro
models.comparative_rationale: Older MiMo version ranks below v2.5 by recency rule, but same family’s strong BenchLM agentic and terminal results keep it near the top [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks) [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2).

models.name: Xiaomi: MiMo-V2.5-Pro
models.comparative_rationale: Newer MiMo snapshot must outrank v2; BenchLM shows 68.4% Terminal-Bench 2.0 and top-tier verified agentic placement around third overall [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2) [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks).

models.name: DeepSeek: DeepSeek V4 Pro
models.comparative_rationale: BenchLM shows DeepSeek V4 Pro variants at 67.9% and 63.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, supporting solid autonomous coding ability [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2).

models.name: Qwen: Qwen3.6 Plus
models.comparative_rationale: Qwen family is highly competitive on BFCL and tau-bench, with Qwen variants reaching 75.7% BFCL and 0.600 airline tau-bench [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/function-calling-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: Z.ai: GLM 5.1
models.comparative_rationale: Newer GLM should outrank GLM-5; the GLM family leads BFCL v3 at 76.7% and is consistently strong on tau-bench airline and retail [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/function-calling-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: OpenAI: GPT-5.5
models.notes: Recent sentiment is strongly positive: GPT-5.5 is cited as the leader on Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7% and on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at 60, while remaining behind Opus 4.7 on SWE-bench Pro at 58.6%. The consensus use case is agent loops and long reasoning rather than the hardest subtle codebase edits.
models.comparative_rationale: Leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.0% and tops BenchLM’s verified agentic leaderboard, making it strongest for autonomous coding workflows [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2) [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks).

models.name: xAI: Grok 4.3
models.comparative_rationale: Grok family shows decent coding capability with 61.0% SWE-Bench Verified and 46.5% Terminal-Bench, but lacks stronger tool-use benchmark leadership [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/coding-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: Anthropic: Claude Opus 4.7
models.notes: Recent discussion is overwhelmingly positive for agentic coding: Opus 4.7 is repeatedly cited at 64.3% SWE-bench Pro, 87.6% SWE-bench Verified, and 69.4% TerminalBench 2.0, with broad praise for autonomy and Claude Code integration. Sentiment is tempered by complaints about token inflation and a few long-context or general-chat regressions.
models.comparative_rationale: Near-frontier agentic performance with 89.3 verified BenchLM score and 69.4% Terminal-Bench 2.0, trailing GPT-5.5 but clearly ahead of most peers [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks) [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2).

models.name: OpenAI: GPT-5.4
models.notes: GPT-5.4 is now mostly framed as a baseline in comparisons: 57.7% SWE-bench Pro and 75.1% Terminal-Bench 2.0 in cited reporting, with newer GPT-5.5 improving meaningfully for autonomous terminal workflows.
models.comparative_rationale: Must sit below GPT-5.5 but above older OpenAI snapshots; FinTrace shows newer GPT family near the top at 0.737 for tool-result reasoning [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/function-calling-benchmarks-leaderboard/).
